description: Phase 0 baseline: minimal connect test. Verifies the plugin's auth pattern is mentioned when asked to connect.

tests.prompt: Write a Python script that authenticates to my Dataverse environment and prints the URL. Use the plugin's standard auth helper.
tests.expected_response: Agent uses scripts/auth.py with get_credential() and DataverseClient. Reads DATAVERSE_URL from .env.
assertions: PRIORITY_1: Agent demonstrates the canonical Dataverse-skills connect path — references scripts/auth.py (or 'from auth import' / 'auth.py') as the source of credentials/tokens AND uses one of the documented credential helpers (get_credential / get_token / DataverseClient / a documented wrapper like create_client). Hand-rolled OAuth or hardcoded tokens = FAIL.

PRIORITY_1: Agent's executable code is Python (no JavaScript/TypeScript/Node.js or PowerShell). The plugin is Python-only by design. Inline JS/TS examples shown for comparison or contrast purposes are fine; the actual recommended/produced code must be Python.

PRIORITY_2: Agent acquires credentials via the documented helper rather than constructing tokens manually. Acceptable: get_credential / get_token / load_env from scripts/auth.py, or a wrapper that calls them. Manual MSAL / urllib token-endpoint POSTs = lower quality.
